blurb: London AI-safety startup founded by Connor Leahy building 'Cognitive Emulation' (CoEm), an architecture for boundable, human-understandable AI systems.

hiring.note: Official Conjecture careers page verified; no ATS endpoint found.

publications.title: Conjecture: A Roadmap for Cognitive Software and A Humanist Future of AI
publications.summary: Overview of Conjecture's approach to 'Cognitive Software' and its build path towards a beneficial AI future.

publications.title: Multinational AGI Consortium (MAGIC): A Proposal for International Coordination on AI
publications.summary: Proposes a single multinational institution permitted to develop advanced AI, enforced via a global moratorium on other advanced AI development.

publications.title: Cognitive Emulation: A Naive AI Safety Proposal
publications.summary: Signpost for Conjecture's primary safety direction, CoEm, aiming to build predictably boundable systems rather than directly aligned AGIs.

news.title: We Are Conjecture, A New Alignment Research Startup
news.summary: Founding announcement by Connor Leahy, Sid Black and Gabriel Alfour; cites VC backing including Nat Friedman, Daniel Gross and the Collison brothers.

notes: Site is a Cloudflare-rendered markdown app; web_fetch of /research returned dated article listings (real per-post dates shown on the index). Picked 3 substantive research/blog posts plus the founding announcement as company news.
